rules.statement: Walking onto some item cells auto-adds the item to inventory (message like 'k - a scroll labeled ...').
Blocked moves come with message "It's a wall." or "It's solid stone." and produce no position/time change. Wall glyphs: '-', '|'. Unlit/unknown cells render as ' '.
Bumping a hostile adjacent creature attacks it: messages 'You kill/hit ...' or 'The X bites/hits/misses!'; damage to us appears as hp decrease (fox bite -1).
Screen layout: tty row 0 = message line, rows 1-21 = dungeon map, rows 22-23 = status. The agent's map cell is tty[y_pos+1][x_pos] and renders as '@'.
rules.scope: checked on reset frames + per-step frames

rules.statement: Floor corpses can be rotted and eating one can poison fatally ('DEATH: was poisoned. Poisoned by a rotted jackal/kobold corpse.', 4 deaths across v7/v8). Rot is invisible in the eat-offer prompt; freshness (recency of the kill) is the only observable proxy.
rules.scope: policy: floor corpses only when desperate or freshly killed, never blacklisted names (poison_corpses.json)

rules.statement: Reaching hitpoints 0 via damage ends the episode with end_reason DEATH.
depth changes only via stair-class transitions (down/up) or unknown rare events; ordinary actions keep depth constant (all 850 probed steps: depth constant).
rules.scope: allow depth+1 on any action as trap-hypothesis until falsified | excludes terminal zeroed frame

revisions.change: scope now excludes the terminal zeroed frame (see R_TERMINAL_ZERO)

rules.statement: Depth can increase WITHOUT taking stairs: 'A trap door opens up under you!' (+1) and 'You fall down a shaft!' (+2). Non-stair depth possibility set must be {d..d+2}. LIVE confirmation: E25 fell 5->7 (shaft chain, t=665) and 10->11 (trap door, t=1175), reaching Dlvl 11.
rules.scope: rare (2 events/741k replay steps; 2 events/24k live steps)

rules.statement: Identical seed => identical initial state and identical transition sequence for identical actions.
'In what direction?' prompts are answered by direction-name actions (e.g. 'south'), applying the pending verb in that direction.
'down' works only on a downstairs tile '>' : elsewhere it fails ("You can't go down here."); on '>' it prints 'You descend the stairs.' and depth increases by exactly 1.
rules.scope: failure branch observed; success branch is pure hypothesis from action name + 'Dlvl' ladder in achievements.json

revisions.change: success branch confirmed: depth 1->2->3->4 at t=52/116/180

rules.statement: Eating a corpse within ~60 steps of the kill is nutritionally effective and was never observed to poison: b3 block, 27 successful corpse/floor eats ('tastes delicious' / 'really hits the spot'), 0 poisonings; occasional non-fatal 'Blecch! Rotten food!' outcomes (light-headedness) from older floor items. Contrast: 4 fatal poisonings all from corpses of unknown age (R_CORPSE_ROT).
rules.scope: freshness proxy = kill recency; rot outcomes non-fatal so far in-scope

rules.statement: 'eat' prompts with edible inventory letters; choosing one restores satiation ('This food really hits the spot!'); with no food: 'You don't have anything to eat.' Starvation causes fainting then death.
'far <dir>' moves 0..N cells along that compass direction (stops at obstacles/objects/creatures); position change is along the named axis only; time advances >= 0.
rules.scope: axis-only displacement so far; corridors may bend paths - unobserved

rules.statement: Some creatures freeze the attacker on melee contact (floating eye 'e': 'You are frozen by the floating eye'); while frozen the agent cannot act and can be killed (E14 death: 'killed by a sewer rat, while frozen by...'). Such glyphs must not be melee-attacked.
The glyph vocabulary is open-ended (e.g. '`' first seen E12 blocking a corridor); passability must be learned per glyph (tiles_learned.json), optimistic-until-refuted, not whitelisted.
0 <= hitpoints <= max_hitpoints; hp slowly regenerates over time (observed +1 after ~14 waits).

revisions.change: scope now excludes the terminal zeroed frame (see R_TERMINAL_ZERO)

rules.statement: blstats hunger_state is an ordinal hunger scale: 1->2 coincides with 'You are beginning to feel hungry' (168x), 2->3 with 'weak' (50x), 3->4 with 'faint' (32x), drops to 0/1 after eating; 0 = satiated ('Satiated' on status row).
evidence: hunger transitions mined from all logged episodes

rules.statement: A locked door ('This door is locked.') can be broken with kick + direction: 'As you kick the door, it crashes open!', after which the cell is walkable.
Kicking empty space can strain a leg ('Dumb move! You strain a muscle.' -> 'Your right leg is in no shape for kicking.'), temporarily disabling kicks.
Killing a creature ('You kill the X!') grants experience_points (fox +4, newt +1).
Letter glyphs on the map are autonomous creatures; hostile ones attack adjacent characters and can reduce hp (observed: sewer rat bite -> hp -1; grid bug bites).

A single-direction action ('north'... 'northwest') either moves the agent exactly one cell in the named compass direction (north = y-1, east = x+1) or leaves position unchanged; never any other displacement. Walking into one's pet swaps places (still a one-cell move).
rules.scope: pos set applies only while depth unchanged (trap falls relocate: E25:665, E25:1175). Rare unexplained exceptions at constant depth: 8/741k replay steps + live E23:413 + b4val_E61:294 (21-cell jump, empty message) ('west' displaced (22,15)->(21,14)); kept as open anomaly class, not patched.

revisions.change: scope now excludes the terminal zeroed frame (see R_TERMINAL_ZERO)
pos possibility set scoped to depth-unchanged steps

rules.statement: A successful single move advances time by 0 or 1; a blocked move advances time by 0.
Named non-movement actions (probed catalog: adjust..zap, wait, search, look, inventory, prompts) do not change agent position.
rules.scope: exceptions expected for teleport-class effects; none observed yet | excludes terminal zeroed frame

revisions.change: scope now excludes the terminal zeroed frame (see R_TERMINAL_ZERO)

rules.statement: The served environment aborts the run as 'ABORTED: quit.' after a long stretch with no game-time progress (observed after zero-time loops: eat-prompt loop E15, peaceful-bump loops E19/E35; matches no_progress_timeout=150 in the official config). Zero-time action loops are fatal to the run.
rules.scope: mechanism attributed to the wrapper's no-progress guard (config.yaml, allowed protocol knowledge)

rules.statement: Some creatures are non-hostile: bumping them asks 'Really attack the <name>? [yn] (n)'. Declining and re-bumping loops forever (E19 lost 3384 steps); they must be treated as obstacles.
A pet creature (observed 'd' little dog, 'f' kitten) starts adjacent, moves on its own, attacks other creatures, and swaps places when the agent walks into it.

Pet glyph classes collide with hostiles of the same class ('d' dog vs 'd' fox/jackal): E7 ignored an adjacent fox as 'pet glyph' and died. Hostility must be inferred from behavior (hp drops while adjacent), not glyph identity.

Bumping one's own pet under the hp-drop heuristic provokes it; a provoked pet fights back and can kill ('Killed by a little dog called Idefix', E37 t=1648 after repeated attack-d swaps). Swap-confirmation must exempt the pet from hostile targeting.

Quaffing an unidentified owned potion can inflict severe damage: VAL_QUAFF (b4val_E60 t=196) 'This burns like acid!' hp 14->6. Unknown potions are not a safe resource; exploit play must not quaff unidentified potions.
rules.scope: single observation; acid class; validation-episode negative result

rules.statement: Praying takes multiple game turns during which adjacent monsters keep attacking: E7 died 'Killed by a fox, while praying' at hp 1. Pray only with no adjacent threat.

Prayer rescue is rate-limited: E21 first prayer cured fainting (t=2008) but a second prayer ~880 steps later got 'You feel that Anhur is displeased' and no rescue; agent died fainted. Prayers must be spaced far apart.
rules.scope: exact cooldown unknown; policy uses >1600 steps

rules.statement: When starving/fainting with no food, 'pray'+confirm ('You begin praying...surrounded by a shimmering light') cures starvation: E3/E4/E5 survived 2600+ turns foodless with 2-3 prayers each, whereas E1 starved to death without praying.

Actions that need an object/direction open a prompt ('What do you want to X? [letters or ?*]' / 'In what direction?'); 'esc' cancels ('Never mind.'). Full-screen text displays (e.g. from 'attributes') swallow named-action keys until 'esc' is pressed. [yn] prompts may need two 'esc'.
Object prompts list the valid inventory letters in brackets, e.g. eat -> 'What do you want to eat? [fghi or ?*]' lists edible item letters.

Feeding raw named-action keys into open menus/prompts can select arbitrary menu entries and even abort the run ('ABORTED: quit.' E15/E19); prompts must be detected and cleared before issuing normal actions.
The starting character class varies across seeds (observed: 'Priest' seed101, 'Monk' seed103) with different stats and inventory.

'Blecch! Rotten food!' outcomes exist that are non-fatal (temporary blindness/light-headedness: 'Everything suddenly goes dark', 'You feel rather light headed'), distinct from the fatal rotted-corpse poisonings.
The agent's own map cell always renders '@', hiding the tile underneath; tiles (e.g. '>') must be remembered from earlier frames. Discovered via E9's 154-step oscillation on the stair cell.
Some monster-letter glyphs are statues: bumping them yields 'You see here a statue of a X.' and no combat; they behave as blocked terrain (E1 wasted 471 attack steps on a lichen statue 'F').

Walking into one's pet swaps places ('You swap places with your little dog.'); the pet is identified by this message. Pets are never valid attack targets (E2 wasted 499 steps ping-ponging with the pet).
On episode end by death, the final observation's blstats are zeroed (time=0, hp=0/0, pos=(0,0), depth=0); other rules' predictions do not apply to this terminal frame.
rules.scope: terminal step only; discovered via 8 anomalies at E1:1030/E2:1857

rules.statement: blstats 'time' is non-decreasing across steps within an episode.
rules.scope: all steps observed so far (850+) | excludes terminal zeroed frame

revisions.change: scope now excludes the terminal zeroed frame (see R_TERMINAL_ZERO)

rules.statement: 'more' and 'space' send raw keys that the game reports as unknown commands; they are no-ops outside prompts/menus.

'up' fails off special tiles ("You can't go up here."); on the starting stair it warns 'Beware, there will be no return! Still climb? [yn]' - climbing up from Dlvl:1 likely ends the run. Replay: 'up' on an upstairs prints 'You climb up the stairs.' and depth decreases by 1 (39x).
'wait' advances time by 0-1 and leaves position unchanged; the world acts meanwhile (pets/monsters move).
experience_level does not decrease (no drain event observed).
rules.scope: weak: xp never changed at all yet | excludes terminal zeroed frame

revisions.change: scope now excludes the terminal zeroed frame (see R_TERMINAL_ZERO)
